title: linaria frutescens
intro: Linaria frutescens, also known as toadflax or butterscotch ragwort, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Western Asia. The medicinal properties of Linaria frutescens lie in its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent, making it useful in treating various health conditions. This plant has been used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as skin conditions like acne and eczema due to its health benefits. Linaria frutescens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, and its roots, leaves, and flowers are commonly used in traditional medicine.
